name: Elyh, Akmakjian
motto: Let's get going.
description: "Ak" decided he needed a college education to practice the great profession of horticulture. He came to Durham for this purpose, and has had enough troublesome experiences to fill a book. If "Ak" hadn't been a hard worker, he might not be with us today, but he proved that he was made of the stuff that wins. "Ak's" main athletic interest has been in boxing. "Ak" is also a prominent figure on the gridiron when there is a soccer game on.

name: Eleanor, Alexander
motto: Thass the way to dew-witt.
description: Alex ambled into Durham (we won't say from where, it might reflect on the town) and acquired a collegiate air with the result that she has almost an intelligent look—witness her picture. Eleanor can't stand bragging. We told her once that we had sprung from a line of piers; she replied, "Well that's nothing, I jumped off a dock once myself."

name: Everett Humphrey, Alexander
description: Why shouldn't Elma be worried about her little boy with the baby face? Although he doesn't seem to give the Co-eds much time, we wonder why he makes an occasional trip to Conn. It's surely not to play his one and only selection on the piano. It's not half bad either. Judging from all sources of information "Pooch" is bound to succeed, as he takes such an interest in all his work (?).

name: John William, Allquist
description: "Johnnie" landed in Durham one fair day on the B. & M. with his umbrella and bird cage. He left his home town in the search for he was the big man. He really comes from Bow—but "Shhhhhhh!!! he doesn't want it known." Soon Johnnie transferred to live in the "Halls of Conant" over which the "Great King" reigns and although he is a charter member of the anti-thermometer club he can now wield a flask in great shape and is master of the retort. Johnnie boasts of the fact that he and Crookes, the famous chemist, went to different schools together.

name: Henry B. Applin
motto: I hit 'em, I do, but not all the time.
description: "Hank", the big, brawny athlete drifted from Providence and took his first boxing lessons in the poster and picture fights. Besides handing out "sleep powders" to the local "Dempsey's", "Hank" is a mean ball player. That is why he was elected to the T. D's. "Hank" would like to play hockey but he says they make the ice too hard.

name: Raymond P. Atherton
motto: Two girls at 20 are better than one at 40.
description: Since Winchester is rather a chilly place, one of its 15,000 (?) inhabitants sought warm abode in the hothouses of the University. His first year in Botany was very successful, probably due to the limited number of microscopes, his being used in partnership. Chemistry 1-a is now his specialty. He is now studying the Bible, being deeply interested in the book of "Ruth". All joking aside, "Ray" is a hard worker and a good friend to everyone.

name: Thomas W. C. Atkinson
motto: A cheerful grin will let you in where the kicker is never known.
clubs: Corporal and Sergeant R. O. T. C. (1), (2)
description: "Atty" hailed from Tilton, a suburb of Belmont, and soon the entire student body was aware of the fact. He was so sure that he wanted to be an engineer that even Math. 9-c could not change his mind. Despite this fact, his cherubic smile offsets his fluent line, and has made him one of our most popular classmates.

name: Sidney Seymour Ayers
motto: To B or not to B, that's the question.
description: The S. S. "Ayers"—tanker, steamed into the port of Durham one morning in September in a heavy fog. However, the fog was not slow to lift and soon Sid was revealed as we see him today. From that day on, he has continued to make many real friends on the campus as well as off the campus. On one of his recent trips he felt real bold and attempted to rob a U. S. Mail car, but was persuaded not to do so by the mail clerk.

name: Barnard, Doris May
motto: Of all the folks I'm liking, Of all the folks who're true, I grow more certain every day There are no folks like you.
clubs: Finance Committee of Y. W. C. A. (3)
description: Doris is all right but she is a little wild at times. Everyone doesn't know what that modest, shy front covers up. Horses are her hobby and her ambition is to become a circus rider. It has been found that with the assistance of Miss Baie, she can do most anything. She is always very much in a hurry - not to get to her destination on time but before time, and that she will win out by this just as did the Tortoise in the race with the Hare. She is a true and loyal friend and nothing if not frank. "Oh, and his hair must be red."

name: Barnett, Isabelle
motto: What a blessing to have ready wit.
description: Isabelle hails from the north country and enjoys many a long ride on the Boston and Maine. Besides being a true country lass, she is a "Home Wrecker". She intends to teach but who can say for how long? We don't know how much of a belle she is in her home town, but we have our suspicions according to the accounts of numerous automobile rides.

name: Bartlett, Francis W.
description: Red came to N. H. right from the top of Plymouth Rock. Others say that he sprang from a cranberry bog, but that is a question still unsettled. He is a man of much business, for, when he is not managing some athletic team, he is writing long business letters to many different people. His fiery temper is only equaled by his red hair.

name: Bean, Joseph D.
motto: Smile and the world smiles with you.
description: Bashful, studious "Joe" started to continue his higher education at Phillips Exeter, but he soon found that it was much nearer home to stop off at the University of N. H., and so we found Joseph taking up his abode in Fairchild Hall, with the other members of the class of '25. "Joe" takes the Engineering course, so we are told, but in spirit of all this hardship he has time to do some "Paging".

name: Bixby, Arthur March
motto: Yoo Hoo Bickie.
description: "Bix" entered the University with the idea firmly planted in his cranium of becoming an engineer; thus far his ambition has been realized, with the exception of one course under Dean Butters. He is extremely partial to "Red" but even now is partly "Khoo Koo". We might add that Bix's good nature makes him a prime favorite at the Estabrook, while his youthful looks are an asset in the classroom.

name: Bloomfield, Benjamin
motto: Somebody else - yessir, not me!
description: From the Rice Institute in Houston, Texas, comes this lanky kid to the class of 1925. Negro lynchings and Ku Klux Klan demonstrations seem to have played an important role in his having bummed his way from Houston to Durham. His appearance in Durham will always be remembered: he thought that the barracks were the sheep barns. Ever since his arrival he has far surpassed his notorious brother Mulligan in the art of escorting the co-eds to numerous affairs.

name: Bolduc, Albert Edward
description: Bunny's masterpiece, "Derry on a Saturday night", will live on forever. However, "Bunny" is best known as the crack catcher for the Freshman baseball squad in the spring of 1922. Prospects look good for the acquisition of the coveted N H by "Bunny" this spring. Here's wishing you the best of luck, "Bunny", from the class of 1925.

name: Bonauto, Louis
motto: Say it with music.
description: "Bonnie" blew in from Wakefield, and has been blowing ever since. He's a little fellar but he can be heard when he takes his trumpet between his teeth. Everybody can tell when he is absent from convocation without looking at the list posted in T hall and his leadership over the horns is missed. His abilities are not confined to music since he is a student and a first class athlete in Tommy's "Gym".

name: Brooks, Joseph John
motto:
A friend you always meet
With a rapid stride of feet,
To the co-eds, he may seem shy
But to the college men he always says 'hi'.
description: After considering the various courses in the catalog, Joe decided to become a chemist. After two years of hard work, Joe discovered his real ability in Math and became a grafter. Perhaps it was because he wanted to become a little more intimate with the co-eds, for once in a while Joe steps out and when he does he certainly makes a flash.

name: Brown, Ralph Everett Tweedy
motto:
Week in, week out, at morn and night
You hear him jazz the ivory keys.
An athlete in love and track.
A fireman of the A. T. B's.
And watch him sling his old slip stick
As he interpolates with ease;
You have to hand it to this youth
At 'Math' he is the cheese.

name: Bryant, John S.
motto: Short of stature but broad of mind.
description: Johnnie blew in from the gate city and had hardly set foot in Durham mud before he began to upset the theory, "Men come to college because it is fashionable." By his perseverance he has become one of the shining lights in the engineering department. Strange as it may seem, the only Co-ed that interests John is the "girl back home".

name: Burnham, Evelyn H.
motto: Never so small, but that we can make some mark in the world." "Say it with olives, or it doesn't mean anything to me.
clubs: Treasurer of Y. W. C. A. (3)
description: Evelyn simply adores olives. Her favorite haunt is the Cabin where she can get "delicious food, daintily served". She has an eye for business, and she manages the Y. W. C. A. treasury funds very creditably. Her major sport is riflery. Considering her several targets, she has a good shooting eye, also. In fact you'll never find her asleep at anything she undertakes.

name: Buxton, Elizabeth Ruth
motto: Hail to thee, blithe spirit.
description: Buckie can do almost everything; her latest achievement being in the line of economics where she has made the discovery that it is cheaper to buy gasoline than to be towed. Buckie is our idea of a good sport and is always ready for anything new provided it has a kick in it.

name: Caldwell, Audrey Lorraine
motto: I would be giving and forget the gift.
description: Like the original wise bird, the more she hears the less she speaks, and hence arises her reputation—What a sweet little girl! Through her "winsome" ways and good sportsmanship she has won much popularity, and from all appearances, she will always "win" it. Whether it is a dare to venture into the icy waters of Oyster River or to bum her way to Dover, she is always ready. We claim she is almost eligible for A. T. B.

name: Campbell, Marshall Fields
motto: "Nig" hates women. Margaret, Abbie and a few dozen others are the only women who have ever bothered him in the smallest way. ("Except those who became too persistent.")
description: Of course allusions to women in GRANITE "write-ups" are not unusual but omission in the case of this young person would have been sacrilege. Many are his virtues. That he started the North Shore Club is his one fault.

name: Carpenter, Charles H.
motto: Blessed be he that slumbers.
description: "Charlie" is one of Manchester High's faithful grads who has gone back to the Queen City every week-end except one, when he had to stay here and manage his Freshmen Cross Country Team. He maintains that the coal business needs him but he has not as yet convinced Prof. Swasey that shoveling coal meets the requirements of Phys. Ed 1-a.

name: Chase, Carl Eddrie
motto: You didn't suppose that he got a 'Jewett' just to ride around in alone, did you?
description: Carl seems to belong to the more (or less) musically inclined as his activities in the band, orchestra, and glee club will show. Not only does he read music notes, but sometimes reads notes from Dover and Connecticut. Perhaps they are musical though. How about it "Chasie"?

name: Chase, Francis
description: Nick gave up his "Ball Square" environment to settle down to a quiet and peaceful life as a student. Fired with ambition he started as a Chem Engineer, but like Gold Medal flour, "Eventually, why not now?" so Nick is enrolled in the college of Liberal Arts. Nick went out for athletics and proved himself no mean athlete but this was before the transition and his career stopped with the coming of midterms.

name: Chickering, Elsie
description: Earnest, Loyal, Sympathetic, Eager. Elsie's Life at College may be compared to a three-act play. In Act 1, the first scene is laid outside the closed door at Prexy's between the hours of 8.30 and 12.00 p.m. The principal characters are "Else" and her own conscience and the climax is reached at the magic word spoken by the man inside. The second scene shows "Else" as a good sport: fishing, tramping, etc. The second act is somewhat allegorical with "Else" playing the part of Sympathy with Anybody. The last act shows her as a confirmed Home Ecker, a confidante, and sympathizing friend.

name: Clark, George B.
description: "Gus" came to New Hampshire from Williston Seminary with a devilish smile and a dignified bearing. He soon fell and joined the "Road Rangers" his first year. Now he wanders to Portsmouth each week-end, and thinks nothing of it,—others do though! It is rumored that he can make a map of the country with his eyes shut. He is conscientious with his books and manages to hit the high marks once in a while.

name: Clark, Kenneth M.
motto: Pigs is pigs.
description: "Ken" answered the call of the wild and left the frozen North of Colebrook, and took up his abode in Durham. Due to practice chasing cows and rum runners this summer he was in the pink of condition and won his letter as a hill and dale man. Rather than break training he keeps in condition by running to Lowell week-ends, also to eight o'clocks. Without a doubt he will be a shining light in his chosen work.

name: Colby, Salome Evalyn
motto: I am a part of all that I have met.
description: "Sal" comes down from the mountains where the trees grow at an angle of 45 degrees with the ground. It wasn't long before everybody knew she was here and she has come to be one of the most prominent Co-eds on the campus, and is a favorite of all.

name: Conant, Dorothy
motto: Firemen, Save my child.
clubs: N. H. Y. P. O. Executive Board (2)
Social Committee Y. W. C. A. (3)
description: "Teddy" caught the one train a day out from the big town and landed safely in DeMerritt Hall. After taking all the Math courses available she decided to elect "Fire Protection" and succeeded in saving Smith Hall and all its inmates from destruction one dark and rainy night. Now she's undecided whether to be a surveyor or cross-continental hiker or both.

name: Coombs, Albert Linscott
description: "Al" graduated with honor from Hampstead High and came here to begin a series of records not unlike the medal list of a Spanish general. But differing from him, "Al" has an aversion toward military life. However, with History as a major subject, the professional world can expect to be benefited by his four years' sojourn at Durham.

name: Coughlin, William Edward
motto: Is that so? I didn't know that!
description: He ran down from the Capitol city and has never stopped. He runs here, there, everywhere. He captained the Freshman Cross Country team, starred in the 440 and 880 in the spring and has been a member of the undefeated relay team for three consecutive seasons, but he does not spend all his time on the track. When a man sharks P. Chem. he must spend a little time over his books. Hard work never goes unrewarded.

name: Cowles, Ethel L.
motto: And the wonder is, that one small head could hold all she knows.
description: You know her. Of course you do, And I bet you are proud of your friendship too. She's the girl you go to when you want something done, And the one you see when you want some fun. A good sport, a leader, a live wire is she— Why she's even a member of the A. T. B.! But to tell dark secrets is not our aim— Instead let's predict a future of worldwide fame.

name: Craig, Annie Kirkwood
motto: That Red-Head Gal.
description: Once she looked in the mirror and tried to count the freckles but gave up in hysterics after numbering those on one side of her nose. She sharks Bacteriology and sets all her friends' nerves on edge by peering expectantly about their rooms in search of bacterium which might be lurking in dark corners. We propose for membership in A. T. B., a good sport.

name: Cunningham, Madeleine
motto: Sympathy is the Law of Laughter.
description: She came to college with a fixed determination of becoming a Home-Ecker. But due to one little Soci course her ambition was fixed to a new and altogether different line. Now she is preparing to reconstruct Franklin according to her knowledge learned in Dean French's classes. Who knows, maybe some day she will be as great or even greater than Thorndike.

name: Cuthbertson, Doris Bertha
motto: True friends, nor death, nor separating fate can e'er divide.
description: Here at the University of New Hampshire we are taught that red is a cheerful color—now we know it! Full of life and spirit, living, translating her good intentions into actual deeds. Whether the road is rough or rocky, Doris is a kind and sympathetic friend. This shows us why she lives so keenly and wears usually so happy a face—she lives not for herself alone but for others, a true "Buddy". Doris! "Oh, my."

name: Davidson, Gaston
description: And she wrote "My dear Gaston"—But to us he is only "Tank". "Tank" matriculated at Dartmouth but realizing his mistake he came down here. This stalwart lad from the mountains of Tamworth, heaves the hammer, and hurls the javelin with no mean form. With his excellent line and his course in debating "Tank" expects to become a second Patrick Henry and those who know him have little doubt.

name: Davis, Philips Shackford Jr.
motto: I hope to meet my N H face to face, When I have cleared the bar.
description: Phil hails from a rugged town way up in the woods, and every now and then, goes home to chase bandits. As yet Phil hasn't caught any bandits, but as for catching a certain mountain nymph, well who knows. It seems Phil acquired his jumping ability running around the mountains and he surely must have had some wonderful "Coaches". On the campus, one will have to look a long ways to find a more likable chap.

name: Dexter, Douglas Hibbard
motto: He hath a daily beauty in his life.
description: "Dex" first came to Durham, back in 1918, when he enlisted in the S. A. T. C. and became a charter member of Company E. After three months of college as a soldier, he enrolled as a Two-Year "Aggie" in 1919. Then he tried dairy farming, and milk-testing in western New York. Finally "Dex" returned to his Alma Mater and joined the Class of 1925.

name: Dolan, Joseph Paul
motto: Cousin Joe comes from the well-known 'Gate City'.
description: Since arriving at our metropolis Joe has made many staunch friends, which shows that without doubt Joe has a bright and brilliant future before him. Here's luck to you, Joe!

name: Drew, Gordon W.
description: One fair day in September, 1921, the "Kid" blew in on the B. & M. from the tall sticks of the state. His first term he heard and answered the call of the pigskin and the horsehide and between the two brought unto himself a cloak of glory. The "Kid" since being disappointed in love is wearing a New Jersey and for consolation is commuting a la flivver to Salem, Mass. On the whole the "Kid" is a pretty good gent.

name: Dyer, Carrol Francis
high school: Middlebury High School, Mt. Hermon School
description: Transferred from Middlebury College 1923. Object—study. Other activities include a part in "Dulcy". Suspected of having broken a banjo by being hit over the head with it. Got out of taking chemistry by leaving Middlebury, now seriously exposed to two History Courses with Prof. Babcock.

name: Eastman, Esther Beane
motto: Love is an incurable Malady.
description: Esther hails from the Queen City and during her Freshmen year she had a very keen fondness for it, but this wore away when she became better acquainted with the university students. "Es", ol' kid, we do wish you would learn to make up your own mind and stick to it so you wouldn't lose so much beauty sleep. Esther excels in Home Economics, that's fine. Maybe there's a reason. Sh! the latest is week-end trips to Rochester.

name: Forrest, Martin Eaton
motto: Who said Earrings.
description: "Mart" never could give the campus much time, being vitally interested in off-campus activities, especially, in "Dear" hunting in the north woods. One time, as a result of his collection of earrings we thought that he would put Woolworth out of business. At present "Mart" divides his attention among boxing, gasoline burning, and possibly studies, but nevertheless is always "laughing".

name: Percy, Alexander Eklund
motto: I can lick any ten men.
description: "Pat" was a reporter for the Attleboro "Balloon" and while hunting news landed in Durham, where he tried out as an engineer, but tripped over the throttle and made a stab at business at Bryant and Stratton in Providence, but he came back and is now making good in the Arts and Crafts. He is a born musician but just discovered it since he got his hands on his Accordion.

name: Earle, Lane Emerson
description: Don't know where Center Barnstead is? Ask Emmy, he came from there - originally. It hasn't been ascertained from whence the name "Emmy" was derived. From his name, one might surmise, but from the statistics of his sophomore social activities other versions might be in order. Who's the fair youngster with the military bearing? That's Emmy. He's been to West Point.

name: John, Andrew Emerson
description: When Booth Tarkington was questioned as to his source of material for "Monsieur Beaucaire" he replied as follows. "At the University of New Hampshire, there is a gentleman of the highest nobility whose real title is the count of Mount Vernon. He impersonated a barber and opened a shop in the Gym. Business was good until 'Red' Pulsifer became a patron. The meeting of 'Red' and the Count was my inspiration," chuckled Booth as he toyed with his monogrammed cigarette.

name: Farnum, Paul Ervin
motto: Get 'em young and make 'em grow.
description: This is Paul. Call him by name when you see him and you are right 50 per cent of the time. Although he intends to be a dairyman he has a leaning for botany and spends his days in the bacteriology lab and his nights holding the palms in the greenhouse. What about the picture on his chifferobe? It has a beautiful frame.

name: Farnum, Robert Bachelder
motto: Get 'em wild and tame 'em.
description: Robert represents 49 per cent of the Farnum twins, being 1 per cent lighter in the hair. Rare indeed is the one who always knows when to say "Robert"; to most it is just "Farnum", which is sure to be right. As a sophomore he came to play a part in quite a lively parade—both as runner and run. The parade sometimes made Madbury via Dover, but it was soon abbreviated. He was then interested in some 15,000 chickens, one being a very "young one".

name: Fenton, Francis
motto: With a punch like the kick of a mule.
description: "Frankie" comes to us from the metropolis of Dover, and has commuted so long that he is about to retire with a pension from the B. and M. He battled his way to fame and starred in the B. U. meet. It isn't hard to get on his list of friends and it is advisable to keep off the dark sheet.

name: Flanders, Franklin
motto: A better friend 'tis hard to find.
description: A straw suitcase in one hand, a hoe in the other, "Frank" blew into Durham on a "Manchester Special" way back in 1920. He worked diligently (?) under Dean Taylor's fatherly guidance for two years, and craving a change, he spent the following year growing oranges in Florida. Returning to Durham this fall, he soon made himself invaluable both to Dan Marshall as a "hash-slinger", and to the Ballard Hall gang as an amateur doctor. "Frank's" versatility is exceeded only by his ambition.

name: Floyd, Iva Sybil
motto: 'Tis so because 'tis so, 'Tis a woman's reason.
description: Iva's friends thought her a girl of a quiet, studious, and retiring nature, until one day we found that she did not have a grinding disposition, as we had supposed, but a — well — an entirely different one. Her life as a Town Girl has surprised us, but that is another story. All in all, Iva is the best of pals, works hard, plays hard, and will, we are sure, meet with success.

name: Ford, Robert
motto: How Merry a Student's Life and Yet How Changeable.
description: Bob left college his sophomore year to fight for his country, then returned to his native hive in Danbury. Soon the call of former associates and Alma Mater prevailed over his official duties as Overseer of Modern Farmer's Cellars and again he traveled the highway in pursuit of knowledge. Between Jack Grant's and Keene, Bob is now continually on the go, and if you have never met him best rotate for a better chap is nil at the University.

name: Foster, Theodore C.
motto: Does he step? I'll say he does!
clubs: B. S. C. Cushing Academy Club
description: Ted blew in with the New Year, leaving Cushing Academy rather unexpectedly and bringing with him a lingo new to these parts. He started to show his athletic ability by making the Basketball team his first term. Ted held down the pivot position on the Varsity and won his first N H.

name: French, Charles Cyrus
motto: Early to bed and early to rise And you never will meet any regular guys.
description: Frenchie hasn't been to bed early since coming to Durham, therefore the sleepy look in his eyes. As a Freshman he roomed in the Barracks and improved the night hours by selling candy. Since then he has spent much of his time doping out schemes to beat Gale Eastman and Red Richardson in the poultry business. Some day we know that he will succeed for he is a hard worker, a good student, and a staunch friend.

name: French, Harold Campbell
motto: For now we see through a glass, darkly.
description: He is majoring in History, and minoring in New York. However, he has the best wishes of those who know him, whatever may be the line of work he intends to take up. And armed with the most tolerant of good natures, he is certain to succeed.

name: Frizzell, Theodore J.
motto: Biscuit
description: With his collegiate clothes and Slim Jim necktie, he escaped from the town famous for its Normal School and upon awakening found himself in the famous town of Bull Durham. In spite of his failing as a woman hater he has been known on certain occasions to break the Lincoln Pledge and step out. When not grinding out a high mark in Agri Chem. he is putting into practice his culinary art.

name: Geremonty, Francis H.
motto: Yes, we have no bananas.
description: He expected to become a farmer but was enticed by the Arts and Grafters. He won the Freshman-Sophomore football game with his "ten baby toes". Ask '26 if he was in the picture fight.

name: Glancy, John Delaney
motto: A Tale of Two Cities—Newmarket and Dover.
description: It was in the dead of winter in the year of 1923, when a tired, footsore wanderer came down the campus lugging a heavy carpet bag, and headed for the Barracks. Jack has made many friends since his arrival but the mystery still remains unsolved as to where he slept during his first three months of college.

name: Goggin, Kathleen Mary
motto: Ah, sweet it is to behold the light of day.
description: "Katha" claims it's a great sensation to pull into Durham before the sun's up, but personally she prefers the comforts of her Ford Sedan when the train is destined to be on time. "Katha's" future is blind at present, but at one time she was dangerously near becoming a "home-ecker". Now, all is changed, however, and she spends all her time madly "Accounting" for lost figures.

name: Gordon, Howard A.
motto: Stub's Sporting Page by 'Dud'.
description: "Stub" is noted for his endurance tests. Without debasement we proclaim him champion "bush" wrestler of the 1925 class. Congratulations to the youth. "Stub" dresses for the occasion and his "Duds" are certainly above reproach. "Stub's" fraternity brothers also proclaim him as champion along other "lines" also.

name: Gordon, Kenneth E.
motto: And I learned about women from her.
description: To see "Speed" going to his beloved eight o'clock classes makes one wonder how he got his nickname. It surely must have been before his enlarging circumference caused him to take such an interest in "Physical Culture". In only one thing is "Speed" an epicure, and that is his choice of tobacco; for he absolutely balks at "Milos".

name: Gould, George Reuben
motto: From the birthplace of prohibition came George with a great ambition.
description: After completing a four-year course at Colebrook Academy little George came to Durham, with the hope of being a doctor. The first operation he performed was upon a cat, the cat died. George has shown ability as a guard in basketball, both on the Freshmen and Varsity teams. Dr. Gould intends to take graduate work at Columbia but we think the Palmer School of Chiropody will claim him, as his feelings tend that way.

name: Gray, Frederick S.
description: Three years ago the statistics from Dad's office showed an unusually large number of potential farmers, one of which is now known on the Campus as "Freddie Gray". Through an unexpected killing of a village cat he discovered his ability to be in other fields than those of agriculture and now the vicinity is being rid of the feline species. The proverbial bee has nothing on this young man for besides studying, editing this book, and winning his letter he carries a heavy social program.

name: Griffin, Elizabeth
motto: "smiles Such as hang on Hebe's cheek And love to live in dimples sleek."
description: Broad grins - understanding hand clasps - a door softly closed - snickers - footsteps - Yes, they're at it again. "What shall we do next?" - warning enough to bring the realization that the rooms in Congreve Hall might be still intact - or might not be. During Freshman year, Beth and her partner in crime, were always under suspicion when anything happened. College, however, has sobering effects - Beth is now a dignified Junior, with only a dimple left to betray her former self. In one thing Beth has not changed - she is, and will always be, the best friend we ever had - to know her is to love her.

name: Groah, Marjorie
motto: "Shan't you die?"
description: Three years ago, Marj grabbed her carpet-bag and started to college with the firm resolution to make large footprints on the sands of time, and we doubt if anyone can dispute this honor with her. Marj has a fondness for hats but then, as she says, she has to have something on her mind. In discussion classes she is at her best. On being asked once her opinion of Chinese bandits, she replied, "Who, the laundry men?" Personality Plus!

name: Hall, Raymond
description: Raymond Hall is a Junior from Dover, who majors in English and considers poetry the most valuable study there is. He has very little to say for himself but just keeps still and absorbs what is going on around him. There's time enough to talk later on.

name: Hammond, Lester Fremont
motto: This is the Forest Primeval.
description: "Les" took his axe in hand and chopped his way out of the wilderness of East Jaffrey, built a bunk at the Barracks and proceeded to get an education. This quiet young Daniel Boone has three deer to his credit. He adopted the Forestry "gang", then studied nature at the greenhouses. His ambition is to graduate and hit the trail for the tall timber. However, "Les" is a true friend and his ability should bring him success in his Forestry career. Brrr — (greenhouse student clerk bell) — D— —those co-eds.

name: Hanney, John
motto: Wait until the 15th.
description: Jack is one of the few federal board men, who are still with us. He deserves all the credit we can give him. After he did his bit in France, he decided to be an engineer and has worked hard to keep the stride set by those whose studies were not interrupted by a little thing like a World War. Jack still looks forward to the first and fifteenth of the month and deserves all the pleasure he gets from his occasional vacations.

name: Hartwell, Arthur I.
motto: I love Me.
description: Once "Speed" thought seriously about going to College but since he lived here he has other things on his mind. He has a pile of friends both on and off the campus. He tried to convince the King that he was a Chemiker and is slowly entering the Chemical field where with consistent efforts he can plant his seeds for the future.

name: Hedman, Sverker N. F.
motto: Silence is golden.
description: Even "Pete's" hair is golden. His worst enemy is a course in English, but we little realize the difference between Swedish and English. "Pete" comes from the wide place in the road above Wilton, but we wonder why it takes him two or three days to get home from the University. Must be some attraction in Boston. However, "Pete" shows good promise of being a clever engineer.

name: Heller, Alta Edith
motto: The girl that can smile is the girl worth while.
description: If you want to find this girl go to Alta. She never worries about her studies. Why should she? "Babe" shows her artistic temperament by the queer array of pictures with which she decorates her room. When you want a true friend go to Alta. She is such a splendid pal, understanding and lighthearted. Anyhow, we feel sure that she will make a success of anything she may undertake.

name: Hersey, Irving W.
description: "Moose" bumped the ties from Somersworth to Durham for a couple of years, but getting sick of commuting he finally decided to take up his abode in Smith Hall, because of his fondness for the fair sex. For the last two years he has labored under the direction of Coach Cowell. Nevertheless we feel sure that "Moose" will make his mark in the Architectural world.

name: Hewitt, Charles Elbert Jr.
motto: All the great men are dead, and I am not feeling well.
description: "Drip" is an Aggie, a real "how be ye" hick from Durham. His father, his cousins, and his brothers are real engineers; now there's no way to explain that except—Oh yes, his father-in-law is a farmer. He's interested in everything, even bees, but he's dropped them now, because the last time he got stung it "Hurt". Let's wish him well for no one knows how he has suffered in the past.

name: Hobson, W. Briant
motto: Height is no indication of intelligence.
description: "Hobby seems to have no hobby unless it be that of an epicure—!" If actions are any criteria of one's remote ancestry, there must have been a firefly in "Bill's" for, now you see him and now you don't. David and Jonathan have a modern counterpart in "Alpie" and Briant until a third party appears upon the scene after which the film somehow fails to pass the censors.

name: Hoitt, Mary G.
motto: A good companion and a true friend.
description: Mary didn't have to come to College. She was right here, but we all found her just the same. Who could help finding a good pal and a good worker like Mary? What is there that Mary can't do? Why she does everything from starring in hockey to sharking in chemistry. She has a host of friends who wish her the best of luck — in short — may the best thing she wants be the worst thing she gets.

name: Holland, Lawrence S.
motto: Earth has not anything to show more fair.
description: Lawrence's favorite expression is "ain't she a dandy". This may be a password of the Walpole Horse-thieves Association, who knows? It may not be known but "Dutch" started as an engineer but found it necessary to shift to the College of Liberal Arts in order to get in the "Library Course" for which he had a fever.

name: Horn, Joseph A.
motto: You're slipping, Joe.
description: "Joe" first made his appearance in 1920, to specialize in the 2-year course, but after six days of strenuous work he decided that a year's rest would be a great help in restoring his lost mental power. "Joe" thinks his high standing in his studies is due to his reputation with the profs. "It's a good thing to have that reputation," says Joe, "because I don't like to study."

name: Hosking, Harry
motto: You must be a basic student.
description: Harry transferred from Middlebury, to become a basic student in chemistry, and has a good deal of trouble in breaking the habits which one forms while studying the lower branches of any science. Now he is one of the few who can juggle retorts and platinum crucibles without thinking about Newton's law.

name: Hudon, Camille A.
motto: Her eyes as stars of twilight fair; Like twilight too, her dusky hair.
description: It's a good suggestion that if you can't take a girl for a ride then "Walker". That's the only thing to do in Durham, and so Camille does it. Camille gave an illustrated lecture on the effect of certain elements combined, which was very enlightening, so we are sure she will be a good teacher since experience is a good, though hard, teacher.

name: Hurford, Archie W.
motto: Even tho vanquished, he could argue still.
description: Archie used to run a Y. M. C. A. in his beloved home town but decided to change his life work to Forestry. In arguing with him concerning Forestry, he shows great favor to any man from the West, nevertheless, he has a particular liking for a certain "East-man".

name: Hurlin, Henry Brackett
motto: Hello, I'm pretty good. How are you?
description: After roaming the high seas for two years, "Hen" decided to lead a retired life in Durham. His training in the navy did not give him any credit in Mil. Art, in fact, he is still taking the course. Three times a year, "Hen" spills his accumulated finances on a certain "attractive distraction" who rigs herself up in queenly fashion for her triennial visit to our university. She especially enjoys tales of adventure on the high seas and our hero generously gratifies these desires.

name: Jenkins, Ellery Wayne
motto: College of Agriculture.
clubs: President Alumni Sons and Daughters Club (3)
Captain of the All-Star Soccer Team (2), (3)
description: In the fall of the year 1921, came the glorious class of '25 to New Hampshire, and with it came a delegate from the "Spindle City", one who has, since his arrival in Durham, made a host of friends, and who has been in the midst of everything going on in class affairs. The following list will show that "Jenk" has not been idle since arriving in town — judge for yourself!

name: Johnson, Harold L.
motto: I must have music while I work.
description: "Johnnie's" ambition is to be an E. E., however much the profs may try to dissuade him. Doc's math was sort of a ball and chain but "Johnnie" wasn't the only one who wore it. "Johnnie" is our "General" and musical aspirant. His taste for music and his ability to play are sources of comment from his neighbors. He is characterized by his pipe.

name: Johnson, Richard Schofield
motto: Goll darn it.
description: After bidding his many sweethearts farewell, "Dick" left the foothills of N. H.'s "young Switzerland" to partake of the joys and sorrows in the institution of higher learning, dwelling amid the shoals of Oyster Bay. He spent two years of his worldly career in Fairchild's "Mausoleum". It was here that "Dick" first discovered his artistic talent by constant use of the "brush" in cleaning Fairchild's "hypostyles". Anyway "Dick" can surely "brush" a mean rendering in architectural design and—bids well to become a foremost architect.

name: Johnson, William D.
motto: Wake me up for a ten o'clock.
description: "Bill" squeaked up from Saugus, and his absence makes a decrease of 50 per cent in the population. He had a reputation as a mathematician but couldn't give the engineers any time. He has tried to establish a reputation as a track man but his studies took up most of his time. Now he is sharking Psychology and shows signs of being a credit to the Economics department.

name: Kelly, Helen Lois
motto: Know any scandal?
description: Kelly, ye olde scandal-monger. She "thrives" on scandal. If you want to be a friend of hers just feed her with the latest bit. Kelly loves children too; and she is never happier than when reciting a certain poem to an appreciative bunch of youngsters. When you feel blue, hunt up Kelly, and her ever-ready wit will have you "roaring" mighty quick.

name: Kimball, Helen Mae
motto: How I hate to get up in the morning.
description: Rosy cheeks and shining eyes. No that's not swearing, there's only one l. Commuter, Home-ecker, that's she. Her choice? Oh there's a reason maybe. She still helps out the Boston and Maine by coming so early on her beloved train. As a friend you'll find none better. Lucky the man who will soon get her.

name: Kizirian, Vaughn Eli
motto: Next.
clubs: Private, Corporal, R. O. T. C. (2)
description: One day in September, a Dodge sedan left a barber's chair and a box of tonsorial operating weapons in front of Fairchild Hall. The "Mid-Noon Flyer" for that day also left a husky little individual, having long flowing hair and four weeks' growth of whiskers. The "worldly" upperclass men thought it was Paul Whiteman, but alas, it was only "Sheik"; a barber, much sought after. After being exposed to Engineering for two years and one term, he became a "Liberal Arts" student.

name: Lufkin, Wilfred Weymouth Jr.
description: The Puzzle. Where does he go? What does he do? When does he study? There are three questions that all Congreve Hall Conclaves have never been able to settle. "He moves in a mysterious way his wonders to perform." Connie might answer, but would she?

name: MacDonald, Harold William
motto: But, everywhere that Lizzie went, Our "Mac" was sure to go.
description: "Mac" entered University of New Hampshire from the "Witch" city of Massachusetts, leaving fair B— to shed hot tears over his departure. "Mac" has a habit of getting away to a late start, but with the co-eds he soon became a sprinter. He learned to lean on Congreve's rail and to stand without hitching like the comic character "Spark Plug", which henceforth became his nickname.

name: Magwood, Alice Anna
motto: A merry heart doeth good like a medicine.
description: "I thought I'd die," says Anne, 'mid merry peals of laughter as she relates her latest exciting adventure. Did you ever know the time when she wasn't ready for a lark? Anne surely knows how to concentrate when she studies, which explains why she has so much time for movies, college games, and her other avocations. We wonder whether this special faculty of hers for "covering ground" was acquired in track or while catching the 8.45 train for Durham.

name: Manikian, Jerayr
description: "Jerry" is one of Tommy's favorite athletes and well deserves his title. He started to live in the barracks but found Fairchild more quiet and has been a Fairchild Fellow ever since. He is undecided as to whether he will continue with Advanced Mil. Art or not but he is always enthusiastic over the proposed hikes so nobody can tell.

name: Mann, Frederic White
motto: A Spanish athlete with socratic tendencies.
description: "Bing" started his career here in the summer of 1923. Formerly a student at Keene Normal School he transferred because of altruistic sentimentalities and mal-adjustments due to environmental conditions. In his present surroundings the only "Mann in the University" has proven himself worthy in all respects as a highly desirable member of the class of '25 by his quiet, imperturbable ways, profound philosophical elucidations, and intellectually aristocratic verbosity. motto: "Go to it, you'll win."

name: Martin, Carl Libby
description: When "Deke" came down from the tall sticks around Colebrook, he brought the old carpet bag and chin whiskers with him. At least six times a week "Deke" receives the best of wishes from his little nurse friend. "Deke" besides being the class flirt, shakes a wicked hand of Penny-Ante. But getting out of the saddle, we'll say that "Deke" was right there in the interfraternity and class basketball.

name: Maxam, Eugene Charles
motto: The test of a man is in playing a poor hand well.
description: After a year's work at Wentworth Institute the "Squire" decided to become a University student, so he registered up on the hill — three weeks late. When it comes to social activities Manchester wins most of his time with Dover fading out in the near background. Besides making good in the College of Technology he is fast becoming proficient in the Art of Military Tactics. 1925 is his class in spite of the fact that we expect him to graduate in 1929.

name: McDuffie, James
description: "Jim" McDuffie is a classmate who has done a great deal for the growth and upbringing of the institution. He spent several summers up in the bushes somewhere between Rochester and Dover, making bricks for Congreve Hall. Among other accomplishments he blows a harmonica and can "go through Georgia" like a bullet through cheese.

name: McIntire, Bradford W.
description: After commuting for a year "Mac" thought that he would "fit" better in town so he decided to take up his abode in Durham. His ability as a chemist has been proven by his separation of the para from paradimethylamidoazobenzene sulphonate of sodium, and proving that its use in bootlegging is no good. Disregarding other good qualities he is a strong upholder of woman suffrage and will someday be a successful stump speaker.

name: McKinley, John
motto: Get 'em while they're hot.
description: "Long John," hailing from New Jersey, landed in Durham, and soon established himself as a dashing end on the '25 football team. When basketball season came around Mac hopped into the center position and since has been a fixture on class and varsity courts. His prowess does not lie in athletics alone for he is a comedian of noted ability and it is a well-known fact that "Gallagher and Shean" got their stuff from Mac.

name: McManus, James F.
motto: Jim came to Durham bringing all the track laurels from Lynn English that he could carry and was bent upon gathering more. It took some time for him to realize that College Track required real training, but soon he starred in the dashes and broad jump.
description: His Sophomore year was broken up by vacations during which he tried his hand as 'Bête de bois'. This year he came back strong, was a member of the winning relay team and now shows good progress towards spring track. Keep it up, Old Man, and you're sure to win.

name: McNally, Gertrude Elizabeth
motto: A voice so thrilling ne'er was heard In springtime from the cuckoo-bird.
description: A confirmed believer in Dr. Twente, Gertie continues to giggle without any noticeable effect. She is of a very enterprising character, her latest fad being the study of a certain element of moving-pictures. Many a night Gertie has been a whole Relief Corps to the starving orphans of the first floor with her cake, candy, and figs.

name: Merritt, Roy Leon
motto: Oh Gee! Oh Gosh! Oh Golly! I'm in love.
description: Roy hails from the "Buckwheat" of Hinsdale, where he ran wild, but upon entering Melrose High received a thin veneer of civilization and a thicker line. Roy isn't very thick, 80 per cent is the passing grade with him, for the rest of us it is 60 per cent. His ability in math is not disputed even by "Doc" himself. We feel sure that he will be a great credit to the University, and someone else in the years to come.

name: Minehan, Samuel Augustus
motto: Novelty is the great parent of pleasure.
description: For two years Sam endured the life of the persevering Commuters. His only amusement was speeding in a Ford Sedan and occasionally the pleasure of chiming in with the "Conant Hall Choir." Sam settled his fame as a dancer when he received a prize at Central Park last spring. He tipped off one of the judges. Now because of Sam's numerous musical and social engagements he is obliged to live in Durham.

name: Merina, V. Morrissette
motto: Her joys in life are three: Her French, Home Ec. and Efficiency.
description: Merina started to be a "Home Ecer" but decided French was more worthy of her ability. Now she can order anything from a French Menu at the Commons or at the Cabin and know what she is getting. Nevertheless "Mareen" keeps up her culinary art as a side line - we wonder why. If you need encouragement go to Merina, for her friendliness and good nature have withstood even the trials of commuting.

name: Paul, A. Morse
motto: She stole my heart away.
description: After conferences too numerous to mention "Mossie" finally convinced the "committee" that he had spent the first fifty years of his life in New Hampshire, whereupon with all due ceremony he registered in the Aggie department where he has upheld the principles of horse sense. On a recent milk-testing trip to Lowell, due to social engagements the milk soured. After all is said and done there is really only one "Mossie".

name: Ida, W. Neil
motto: Westward, the course of the empire.
description: Ida is so quiet and dignified, but that is one reason we think so much of her. Another is - well, just ask Ida to sing for you. Singing isn't her only accomplishment, however, for she can play - and how she can dance. Her fame has even reached the West - certain parts.

name: Austin, Sibley Norcross
motto: Why walk to Manchester, when one may ride on the Ireland Special.
description: How "Bob" conceived the idea that he was an engineer nobody knows, but once started he is now pushing through with his usual forcefulness. Upon graduation he expects to find use for his talents and we feel sure that we may see him clean up (?) in some of our big engineering industries. He is now taking special courses from "Irene".

name: Norton, Louise
motto: To know her is to love her.
description: Louise came to us from the class of 1924, having spent part of last year at Simmons, but she finally had to come back to Old New Hampshire. We were all glad to hear and see her when she registered two weeks late last fall. Even now Louise does not give us too much of her time — she goes home for week-ends frequently, her definition of a week-end being from Friday night to Wednesday.

name: Noyes, Beatrice Ellen
motto: Never do today what you can put off till tomorrow.
description: Afternoons "B" usually airs her views on all the latest scandal. Formerly she was winner of the Indolence honors but her enthusiasm in winter sports both indoors and out seems to eliminate her entirely this year. As a congenial and hearty roommate she cannot be beaten. She surely is a jolly good pal for "a' that an' a' that."

name: Noyes, Everett A.
motto: A thinker, a worker and a good judge of Co-eds.
description: Everett came to Durham with the idea of sharking the engineering course but after taking a make-up in Physics 7-b he decided to drift along with the rest of his classmates. Nevertheless he surely hits the books. We often wonder what those letters postmarked Lisbon contain. However, we wish him success whether in engineering or matrimony.

name: Nutting, Louise
motto: She's pretty to walk with, She's witty to talk with, And pleasant to think on, too.
description: Efficiency in everything she does is the keynote in Louise's song of life. Her favorite indoor sport is crabbing the Commons, so she will surely appreciate her Senior privileges, in spite of the fact that one will be unnecessary. No one can deny Louise does possess versatility, she is as proficient in all branches of Home Ec., from chasing minute bacterium, to managing the family budget, as she is adept in sports, especially in winter sports when "Snow" is around.

name: O'Gara, Edward James
motto: The fist is mightier than the sword.
description: "Doc" remained out of the limelight his first term but as soon as Coach Cohn called for the boxing candidates "Doc" was the first to shuffle the dust off the new ring. Since then, to the delight of Harvey Cohn "Doc" has met all comers and has been victorious. Social activities offer no inducements to this boy from Hanover - but who knows?

name: O'Kane, Elizabeth Wells
motto: To look up and not down, To look forward and not back, To look out, and not in, and to lend a hand.
description: "Yes, she's always there with a ready word of sympathy and encouragement - whether one's bad news is a call down from the Dean, or merely a "K" in one of Mr. Alexander's difficult courses. Being game clear through, she slides over her bumps along Life's way without a word of complaint and gives forth her theory, "Laugh at them."

name: Page, Emily Wills
motto: A merry heart doeth good, like medicine.
clubs: Member Sons and Daughters of Alumni
description: An alarm clock, a spool of thread, a bank full of pennies - ordinary enough to our eyes - but to "Em" - what infinite possibilities they hold. "Em" was the original practical joker, and you who have not been the victim of her pranks have indeed missed much. If you ever feel down and out or bored to death go see "Em", and you will decide that life is worth living in less time than it takes to tell about it.

name: Paine, Florence Alice
motto: Strewing the path with smiles.
description: Florence's sunny disposition was not harmed in the least by teaching; in fact we wouldn't believe she had taught, if she hadn't told us. Her hobby is playing with spoon and rolling pin. She can make better fudge than any Home Ec-er. We have inside information that her interests are divided between Durham and Madbury, where she spends many a weekend.

name: Partridge, Eva Small
motto: Moderation is safe.
description: "Pat" is the only "speed violator" the Commuters' Organization can boast of. Of traffic laws, she has no regard; in fact her sole respect for laws of any kind is centered in the classroom. "Pat's" sole ambition is to enter business. Every day her plans materialize more favorably, and she is now fully prepared to become an active member in a Co-partnership—to be formed some time in the future. Through "Pat's" athletic efforts, our class has ever been a victor.

name: Peterman, Gustave C.
description: "Pete" came to New Hampshire with a few years handicap over the rest of us but didn't lose any time joining our ranks. He started right off and was our first president. He filled this office so well that he was re-elected and was a great asset to the class. We notice that he doesn't give the co-eds any time unless he wants something done but this is easily explained when you see him taking his family for a Sunday afternoon ride in his Hudson.

name: Pettee, Donald A.
description: "Dean" was the big cheese in his home town, being station agent, telegraph operator, cop, mayor, gate tender, express agent, truant officer, and dog catcher, incidentally clock-winder. Since "Dean" left home these offices have been left vacant but he still says, "U' Needham." He will no doubt be a great financier due to his ability in levying fines, but nevertheless he will make some man a good wife due to his experience in handling a big family.

name: Pijouhy, Russell A.
description: "Peach" came to us from over the seas after the disastrous fire in Smyrna. He is somewhat of a traveler, having seen every continent except Australia, but he doesn't expect to return because, as he says, "I saved my neck once." He is a shark in Languages and can speak six without trying. As a result he has forgotten which is his own. He has several names on the campus but Mr. Lowry is the only one who has the right one.

name: Piper, Harold Avery
motto: Pipe was found drifting around Great Bay on a barrel, (hence the bowlegs). He was at first very bashful and wild but the boys tamed him down a bit and he is now nearly civilized. Once, after the boys had dressed him, washed him and combed his hair he condescended to have his picture taken. 'Pipe' has made a man of himself on the gridiron and has rightly earned the name of 'Dynamite' and we all look forward to seeing him smashing through.

name: Pray, Eleanor Frances
motto: A merry heart maketh a cheerful countenance.
description: After one year Eleanor decided that she couldn't give Bates any more time and so came to New Hampshire. Her first year at Durham she spent taking week-end trips to Lewiston, but all that is changed and she is now sharking all the "Jim" courses. "Ellie" is always to be found either on the Smith Hall porch or at the library — her major requires a lot of outside work. No one has ever found "Ellie" very talkative but then — "still waters run deep."

name: Putnam, Pauline
motto: Yes I'll go — if I don't get another invitation.
description: After spending a year at B. U., "Polly" decided to finish her college career at the University of New Hampshire. Did you ever see "Polly" when she wasn't planning to go somewhere? Most of her spare time is taken up by dances, movies, etc., besides answering her various letters from Boston and — Oh yes, "Polly" is a good sport and we are all crazy about her and she really does find time to do her studying.

name: Rand, Harold Tinkham
motto: Behold 'The Gawk'! Student, athlete, and good friend par excellence. His successes in one activity are excelled only by his achievements in others. 'Tink' is noted for his specs, night cap, and favorite sayings, also for his love of old Salem and other things. 'In Unity there is strength' used to be his motto but it was recently changed to 'In God we trust'.

name: Rasnick, Julius
motto: At last 'Jules' is in print where a close-up can be obtained.
description: He does not perform much on the Campus, but every son of the Hub has a specialty in some line or other. 'Jules'' is studies and oriental dancing. He came here first with '24, but was forced to take a vacation but has now returned after a long illness and we hope that the future will bring health and success in his chosen profession.

name: Redden, John Daniel
description: 'Pete' dropped into Durham one day in September, a few years ago. He was driving a Ford, and busily engaged in making a very black corncob pipe function. 'Pete' is a student while at Durham, but in Dover, he is hailed as the foremost poultry merchant of that metropolis. 'Pete', due to his remarkable popularity with the fair sex, has acted as official 'fixer' at the Commuters' Ball.

name: Reynolds, Fred Irving
description: Although Ted is generally known as 'Chink' he might better be called a Red because of his radical actions. He is a smooth worker, a very innocent type and always manages to place the blame on somebody else when the rooms are stacked. 'Does Ted live in the Kappa Sigma house or the library?' is a question that has not been decided. The problem became more complex when we found that he was not a student.

name: Ried, Edith
motto: My mind to me a kingdom is.
description: Way back in '21 there was one Edith Ried who came with us and launched out on a varied schedule. She soon found that history was her calling and she has sharked her courses in it ever since. England is Edith's hobby, and if you want to hear positively yards of poetry about said country, just call on 'Bob'. All of Edith's friends, however, will vouch for the fact that playing the 'cello, and taking a twenty-two hour schedule are not enough to keep her busy. The rest of the time she spends arguing with her friends, for not the least of Edith's accomplishments is her persuasive ability.

name: Riley, Mary Elizabeth
motto: Life is real, Life is earnest.
description: "Rile" is just crazy about having at least eight o'clocks. Who wouldn't love to get up with the birds and crawl into Durham (a la B. & M.)? "Rile's" favorite occupation is to keep the rocking chair in motion. Mary's not a man-hater. Oh no! She's given more than one fellow the "bounce". Everyone who knows Mary will agree that she's a good sport and a real friend.

name: Robinson, Ruth
motto: One's only an aggravation.
clubs: World Fellowship Committee in Y. W. C. A. (3)
description: Ruth is taking the Home Economics course, expecting to teach; but we are wondering how long she will keep up that profession. She is very good at cooking, so we will probably be reading in the Alumni notes that she is making cakes for some nice man in Rhode Island. The trouble is, Ruth keeps most everything to herself. We never knew until lately what a jazz artist Ruth is, because she, for one, can keep a secret.

name: Rollins, Willard D.
motto: Down the Lakeshore Line an Altonite blew, And from his belt a trumpet drew, From then on all Durham knew . . . .
description: "Pete" dropped down from Alton, back in 1920. He then returned to the village on the bay for a year and again matriculated to the University with a wonderful repertoire. As a runner "Pete" is there with the goods and as a member of the musical clubs he has done much towards their growth and development. Nevertheless "Pete" has time for many week-end trips to Warner, where he says he has to play in an orchestra.

name: Sampson, Donald L.
description: "Don" was rather quiet during his first year at New Hampshire, but later developed a mania for run-down flivvers and he sure makes plenty of noise now. He now stands high in managerial competition, and with the managership of Freshman Track he stands well on the way towards winning his N. H. as varsity manager.

name: Sanborn, Daniel Bradbury
motto: Since this young Sampson with the pleasant smile first came to Durham, he made them all sit up and take notice by the way he cut up on the football field. When it comes to business, "Jock's" right there with the hot-dogs. It would be hard to find a better or more agreeable friend than "Jock" and if this counts for anything he will be sure of becoming successful in the world.

name: Sanders, George E.
motto: "Fat" landed in Durham from a T. B. Sanitarium, a mere weakling of a lad, but due to the nurses at the Lowell General, he has become a robust and portly gentleman. "Fat" does everything from tickling the ivories to swabbing dishes and his touch in both instances is very delicate. In spite of the fact that Dr. Howes does not appreciate his ability and agree with his theories he is due to make a successful engineer.

name: Sargent, George Eaton
motto: "Doc" is a profound woman hater, but, we don't believe it; "Doc" was to be an engineer but a pick and shovel didn't appeal to him and Arts and Science was in order. Some day he will be an Economist and will make his pile in Wall Street.

name: Sargent, Lloyd Gilman
motto: "Sarg" breezes in every morning on the flying 8.05 which stops at Plaistow and all way stations. His early training in dodging through the crowds of Plaistow to catch this train easily enables "Sarg" to make his class football team as a regular. Lloyd tried living in Durham for a while but the call of nature was too great and once more he has become a popular member of Plaistow society.

name: Sawyer, John Thomas
motto: Centimeter Junior,
description: Received the brilliant inspiration along in his childhood days that he had the qualities of an engineer. His marked ability in physics won a place for him in this department even while a Junior. Because of his quiet, unobtrusive manner his value has not been appreciated, but among men watch his smoke.

name: Sayward, William Sewell
high school: Wayland High School, Browne and Nichols, Cambridge Latin
motto: The Primitive Brute.
description: A big manly, square shouldered, clean fisted type. A type for masters of fiction to develop. A person who seems to embody in one individual the spirit of the great open spaces and the character of a super man. However, he's just a student, even as you and I, and the only open space he knows is the private little clearing in the College Woods. Boston is near his home town of Wayland.

name: Scott, Don Pitt
high school: B. M. C. Durfee High School
description: Scotty is a widely-traveled young gentleman - he admits it himself. Opinions on such delicate subjects as love, wearing apparel, graft courses and the world at large are answered by this information bureau whether you include return postage or not. Even at that you must admit that he is an "authority" on most of the things he talks about.

name: Seaman, Ralph Henry
motto: Good things come done up in small packages.
description: Behold the future director of vocational education! He may not know what it means but nevertheless "Alpie" goes to Newmarket twice a week and indulges in teaching the young hopefuls a few of the points gleaned from "Batch". Once "Alpie" makes up his mind, try and budge him - might as well tackle the proverbial mule. Our mutual friend hopes to lead the soccer team to victory if he doesn't break his neck trying to follow "Mick".

name: Shea, Edward A.
description: "Yebbs", as he is familiarly known, is without doubt one of the most popular fellows in school. He was an active and hard worker both for the class and for the institution. His amiable character coupled with a convincing line made many friends for him. This was clearly shown when he was elected captain of the Fairchild Hall baseball team, and president of the Nashua Club. He is the kind of a gent that everybody wants to be able to call a friend. We all extend to him our best wishes for a bright and successful career.

name: Shepard, Morrill Francis
motto: Ain't love grand.
description: In "Shep's" first year at the University his numerous trips to the home town caused a slim pocketbook and many a heartache. However, "Pep" has now reached a more mature age and "Haverhill" frequents his thoughts to a great extent. As a clerk, and a messenger for Uncle Sam he has no equal but he is still looking for his bicycle from Sears Roebuck Company. On the campus he is a puzzle, but to those who know him well he is a great sport and a wonderful friend.

name: Simonds, George W.
description: From whence cometh this youth With complexion so fair, Whose presence always stirs Music in the air? From the M. H. S. class of '21 came George Simonds, with his ever ready wit and attractive personality. He won a host of friends. The E. E. course seemed to be to his liking so he continued with the course until the beginning of his Junior year, when he changed to Industrial Engineering. George did not recognize his talents in elocution or he would now be among the "bright lights" of Mask and Dagger.

name: Simpson, James S.
description: "Jim" transferred from Bates this year. In that Institution he showed marked ability as a track man. He is not eligible to take part in his favorite sport here. But "Jim" is not the only one that transferred either. Oh no, he does not commute, although one does see him at the station quite often when the 6:16 is due.

name: Smith, William Alfred
motto: "Friends, Romans, Countrymen," with these imposing words "Bill" addressed the family horse.
description: This was only his start in life and from then on he decided to pursue his linguistic desire at the University of New Hampshire. Two years of hard study passed by, during which "Bill" proceeded to gather in scholastic honors. His new introduction became: "Mr. Chairman, Ladies and Gentlemen" - but why proceed, "Bill" won the debate and many others.

name: Sneierson, Morris Frank
motto: "Yee-e-e-e-e-e! Big time tonight!"
description: Immediately upon his arrival in Durham, he created such a stir among the co-eds that the R. O. T. C. promptly drafted him for the army to save the poor girls. However, we have cause to believe that his interests are still in Boston. A great desire to help others seems to be Mose's most favorable trait. He is popularly known as "Teachers' Pet," and "Professor of the Barracks."

name: Snow, Martin
motto: "A race well run is a race well done."
description: "Marty" once chased cows in Littleton, but finding the four-footed animals entirely too slow for him he left the wilds and matriculated at the University of New Hampshire where he proceeded to attract the eye of Coach Cohn. He has won his letter for three consecutive years on the Cross Country Team and has twice been elected captain.

name: Summerville, George Herbert
motto: "I'm the Sheik of Araby, and all the girls, they fall for me."
description: George drifted into Durham in the fall of '21. Because he was a very green freshman, he decided to be a Chemiker. His courage was good so he kept on and may yet be a real Chemist. Chief among his activities are understudy to Mrs. Oliver and dancing. As an all-round sport and a good friend he commands a high mark.

name: Spaulding, Ernest Frank
motto: Great men speak but few words.
description: "Waldo" is best known as a dry humorist, and as such he has Will Rogers lashed to a mast. "Waldo" has never been guilty of being seen with any of our fair co-eds but many of us have our suspicions - you know, if you believe a thing it is so. A rumor is about that "Waldo" has been chosen to design the building to replace Pettee Block; if this is so a masterpiece is assured.

name: Sprague, Roger Edmund
description: Roger was among the ranks of the commuters for the first term but couldn't see getting up at five o'clock every morning during the winter term so joined the Barracks Gang where he stayed until he was given a vacation by Dame Scarlet Fever, during the spring of '23. He is now spending his time hunting graft courses in order to graduate with the good old class of 1925, which we are certain he will do.

name: Stearns, Glenn Aetherton
motto: Big, Rugged, Handsome, Cave-Man.
description: They're all the same to our mighty Glenn. The former has surely served the purpose on the campus, while on vacations Glenn starts out in a Ford, and usually ends up in a Stutz. Some might call him quiet, but those who know him seem to vote in favor of a young "Cyclone". On the athletic field Glenn swings a wicked hammer, also puts a long-shot, and usually gets "Daisies" as a result.

name: Stockwell, Ira Worcester
motto: Love is not love, which alters when it alteration finds.
description: An ordinary person is now socially ostracized for even mentioning anything about "bananas". Somehow, however, as "the pup" sings his incessant, heart-breaking plaint, we haven't it in our hearts to say a thing against him. To be disappointed in love is to him almost as bad as to be a member of the "Purity League". A musical temperament, fear of the dark, alarm clocks, and Marlborough, characterize him in a sentence.

name: Sullivan, John Patrick
description: "Sully" came into our folds in the fall of 1921, after serving his apprenticeship as a manufacturer of ladies' high class shoes in the "Queen City". "Sully" has a knack for making friends wherever he goes, and he is always in the midst of everything — even studies. As Grind Editor of this book he has shown the stuff he is made of, and his success in after life is well assured.

name: Talbert, Elmer James
motto: Beware the fury of a patient man.
description: "Tubby" first came to the University of New Hampshire with the intention of becoming an industrial engineer. At the end of his sophomore year, however, he transferred to the Arts Course in Chemistry. He has often proved his superiority with the "mitts" in "old 203", and upon occasion, he is equally superior with the mop and pail. Hard working and persistent in anything which he undertakes, "Tubby" has won the admiration and affections of all those who know him.

name: Tamcales, George H.
description: George is a descendant of Hippocrates; hence his love to fool with skulls, bones, arms, legs and — "everything". His father wanted to make him a country gentleman but George ran away to America to become a doctor. George went to K. U. A., where he graduated. Later he went to Harvard, but when he discovered that a month's expenses there could keep him alive a year at U. N. H., he hastened hither — and he's on his way to become a surgeon. In U. N. H. George married Miss "My-Goodness".

name: Temple, Earl S.
motto: Remember the prune was once a plum.
description: In the fall of '21, "Temp" tore down to Durham and landed after his Dodge had stopped to his customary "Whoa!" His avowed purpose, strange as it may seem, was to complete his education. "Temp" comes from a town near Penacook, called Concord, the home of the Legislature. With such intellectual surroundings, this boy should be heard from soon.

name: Thurston, Dorothy Chase
motto: For she is wise, if I can judge of her, And she is fair if that mine eyes be true.
description: "Dot" shines in riflery and Home Economics. You may not be able to get the connection, but let us tell you that put together, they're a killing combination. If she doesn't reach their hearts one way, she does in another and those childish charms finish the deadly work. "Dot" also qualifies for the A. T. C. in addition to her other activities.

name: Tinker, Jeanie Mildred
description: "Jempty" came to us from Manchester, and immediately "played" her way into popularity. For two years, she was Mrs. Wassall's right-hand man. Then add to her ability as a pianist, that of a dancer of rare quality, and a warm Scotch heart, and we present you "Jempty" - altogether one of the best friends we know.

name: Tirrell, Alice Dorothy
motto: We leave our work when she returns And run to hear her talk.
description: There was tumult in the Commons at night and during quiet hours too. And when peace reigned supreme again, there stood our head proctor, her perpetual smile beaming at us, and she was saying calmly, "Oh yes, I'm going to give all of us black marks." Alice steps out often - so often that recently she wore out a new pair of shoes walking with - but she says we needn't dare to mention "Barney".

name: Tuttle, Eleanor Jane
motto: The magic of the tongue is the most dangerous of all spells.
description: We always thought Jane would make an ideal wife until we saw her "gumming up the works" as the star in "Dulcy". "Well, you know how 'tis," she says, apologetically, as she brings home her humble 90's in History. No, we don't, but we wish we did. Try to change her mind sometime, - you can't - but don't try not to like her, - you can't do that either.

name: Twombly, George A.
motto: Worge drove his steamer, a vintage of '90 — doesn't matter what century — to Durham in the fall of '21, with aspirations of securing Dean Crouch's position. (Now it is Dean French's.)
description: Things were beginning to shape up, thought George, as he emerged from the Poster-Fight with a rapidly swelling eye, but he has since proven his mettle and has managed to stay in — or should we have said come back to college each term?

name: Varrell, Merton Wentworth
motto: I'm gonna flunk out this term.
description: The good looking Swede is another Portsmouth product. He's the only man known to pass seventeen hours after receiving as many hours warning, and he is one chemist who has not been choked with poisonous gases. Mert may be homesick, nobody knows, but he visits the Navy Yard town about every week-end and as Post says, "There's a reason."

name: Viola, Louis Victor
motto: Pardon my inexperience, now what do I do.
description: In years to come we expect "Vie" to crowd Michael Angelo out of the public mind. He is better than "Mick" when it comes to drawing from the pater's pocket book. We wonder if "Vie's" grey locks are due to hard study. They are unique, to say the least. "Vie's" future is assured if he continues as he has begun.

name: Walker, James Edward
description: "Jimmie" bade the old folks fond adieu, mounted the aged fleabitten mare and shimmied down New Hampshire highway to Durham. "Jimmie" was a very meek, curly-headed youth during his Freshman year and Informals were an unknown quantity to him. Then came his Sophomore year, — "Evolution" — is the word. From Youth's Companion to Love Stories went our fair youth, via campus society, and he soon became one of the numerous fashion plates. At present, "Jimmie" has nearly finished his social thesis entitled, "My 'Bon Ami' is back in the mountains, but sweet 'Woodbury' lives near the sea."

name: Walker, Susan A.
high school: Newmarket High School, Durham, N. H.
motto: Your wit makes wise things foolish.
description: When "Sue" started her career at this college, she immediately began throwing a line to the professors, catching high marks as well. She ardently patronizes the local movie theatre, and entertains her companions with her ready wit. She is firmly convinced that the distance from her house to "T" Hall is three miles, but the guideboards say but one.

name: Wallin, Carl Gustaf
high school: Brighton High School, Huntington School, Brewster Academy, Northeastern University
description: "Porky" Wallin is a very sophisticated young man. His reputation as a good fellow is known from Bar Harbor to Jekyll Island and as far West as the Pacific Ocean. "Porky" has found Durham rather quiet and has never been able to settle down for more than one term at a time but like the bad penny, he always comes back. We wonder if Carl had the same convincing methods of argumentation when he lived among the statesmen in Washington, D. C.

name: Warren, Edward Lyman
description: It was with much compunction that "Eddie" tore himself away from Laconia, for "Lucretia" spent her summers there. "Eddie" displayed his artistic technique by drawing her summer cottage on his banjo mandolin and has contumaciously played "Home Sweet Home" on it ever since. "Eddie" is also a statistician. If you must know a fact with nicety and precision ask "Eddie". His syllogisms are infallible. He has pursued erudition indefatigably but hasn't overtaken it.

name: Wason, Bernard Albert
motto: Toiling, rejoicing, sorrowing, Onward through life he goes.
description: "Bernie" came to Durham very silently and has been a mystery man ever since. The mystery of his frequent visits to Berwick, Maine, was revealed when he was seen walking the street of Durham with a "Fair one" leaning on his arm. He has been faithful to everything which he has undertaken and we are sure that he will succeed.

name: Wentworth, Shirley P.
motto: "Si" hails from the Witch City where he was the mainstay of the high school backfield.
description: At New Hampshire he has developed into the best all-round athlete in the history of the College, having won letters in all major sports excepting cross country by the end of his sophomore year. He captained the varsity football team his junior year and has been reelected for the season of '24. The Class of '25 is justly proud of him as the biggest athlete of the University of New Hampshire.

name: Wheeler, Kenneth A.
motto: "Pullmans"—"Porters"—"Women"
description: Jack being a quiet lad by nature, surely kept all his outside activities sort of dark, until the fall term of his Junior year. Ability to get acquainted with certain people, so quickly in fact as not to give his companions a show at all, grew over night. After a trip out west, Jack stepped off the train at Durham and in all earnest, yelled, "Porter". Nevertheless, Jack is a true friend to everyone, and is always there with advice on all questions.

name: Wiggin, Elmer Stoddard
motto: "Wait a minute, I'm coming."
description: "Chip" is one of the achievements of the town of Hannah Dustin. Possessing her courage and versatility he enrolled as an engineer, but soon became attracted by the "economic" aspect of Smithy's Arts and Graft course. In addition he now studies Astronomy so that he can better appreciate the "Milky Way". Nevertheless, he is a good scout and if ever in Penacook, see the Baby Elephant at Contoocook River Park.

name: Wilder, Parker S.
motto: "Ever faithful to one, he has no eyes for others."
description: "You know that don't cha", and we did, for Parker has been trying to let us know it since he dropped off the local from Newton, with a fiddle under his arm, Anne saw that he must strive to conquer. When this youth doesn't mistake Congreve Hall for his "frat" house he is generally found hitting his books in his study or displaying his ability to sing, act or use the fiddle and bow at the gym entertainments.

name: Woodbury, Marjorie Helen
motto: A miniature of loveliness, all grace Summ'd up and closed in little.
description: "Marge" first came into the college limelight by being elected secretary of her class; besides that, we feel sure that if the class were balloting for the best "Walker", she would surely win the vote. "Marge" is the business woman of her fraternity. This doesn't mean she dislikes a good time for no dance would seem quite complete without her.

name: Woodman, George B.
motto: His bark is worse than his bite.
description: "Woodie" tied himself and his piccolo to a piece of driftwood and sailed down the Pemigewasset from Plymouth in the fall of 1921, and in a few months found himself on the shores of Oyster Bay. From there he wandered to the registrar's office and became a member of the loyal class of 1925. Packers Falls had much attraction for him at first but he soon lost his heart to a "Mexican Rose".

name: Young, Sumner D.
motto: Still waters run deep.
description: One morning in the fall of 1921, this tall lanky youth first stepped on the campus. During his first two years his trips home were very frequent but "Howe" they have changed is a mystery. At present the rough-houses at Fairchild have ceased and now he is devoting his time to Dean Crouch's department. Although not a shining light on the campus, "Sum" is an all-around good scout.
